title: Walkthrough: Able Assess Falls Risk Tests

seo.title: Walkthrough: Able Assess Falls Risk Tests | Able Care
seo.description: Four validated falls risk assessments in five minutes — grip strength, lower-limb function and balance — captured by any staff member.

blocks.headline: Four falls risk tests. One five-minute screening.
blocks.subtitle: Run by any staff member. Validated against CDC STEADI and the 2022 World Guidelines for Falls Prevention. No clinical certification required.
blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Able Assess running on a tablet next to the GripAble sensor

cards.title: The kit
cards.body: A GripAble sensor, a measuring tape, and the blue fanny pack that holds the sensor during the gait, balance, and chair-stand tests. Ships ready to use.

cards.title: The Able Assess app
cards.body: Walks any staff member through every test, step by step. Fifteen minutes of training, no clinical certification needed.

cards.title: The Able Assess portal
cards.body: See risk stratification across your population, and track changes for any individual over time. Export reports for care plans, family meetings, or audits.

metrics.name: Single Maximum Grip Strength
metrics.whatItMeasures: Peripheral marker of muscle mass, bone density, and biological resilience. The Lancet (2015) showed grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

metrics.name: Timed Up & Go (TUG)
metrics.whatItMeasures: Dynamic balance and functional mobility. One of the most widely validated falls risk tests in the literature, captured in under a minute.

metrics.name: Four-Meter Gait Speed
metrics.whatItMeasures: The 'sixth vital sign' for older adults. Linked to mortality, hospitalization, and functional decline. Distance is set with the included measuring tape.

metrics.name: 30-Second Chair Stand
metrics.whatItMeasures: Lower-limb strength and endurance. A direct indicator of fall likelihood and a leading marker of functional decline.

steps.title: Measure Grip Strength
steps.description: The participant squeezes the GripAble sensor when prompted. The app records the maximum reading and stratifies it against age-banded norms.

steps.title: Lower-limb assessments
steps.description: Slip the sensor into the fanny pack. The app walks you through the Timed Up & Go, four-meter gait speed, and 30-second chair stand — all using the same sensor.

steps.title: View outcomes
steps.description: The app stratifies the participant into low, intermediate, or high falls risk. Results sync to the Able Assess portal for trend tracking and reporting.

cards.title: Direct
cards.body: Co-branded communications help seniors understand recommendations from the CDC STEADI protocol — and the services available in their community.
cards.linkText: TODO: swap this stand-in image for the 'AA. Next Steps – Segment Generic' export from Google Drive

cards.title: Report
cards.body: The assessment report is defensible documentation for a referral to physical therapy or other preventative care, as directed by a primary care physician.
cards.linkText: TODO: confirm /downloads/falls-risk-profile.pdf is the right example PDF

cards.title: Monitor
cards.body: View screening data over time in the Able Assess portal to understand improvement or decline at the individual and population level.

blocks.heading: Want to understand how it works in your setting?
blocks.subheading: Able Assess adapts to your environment. Pick your setting to see the segment-specific case.

segments.title: Home Care
segments.description: Reduce client falls, standardize across non-clinical caregivers, and protect HHVBP performance.

segments.title: Senior Living
segments.description: Protect residents and occupancy. Objective screening across IL, AL, SNF, and CCRC.

segments.title: Pharma & CROs
segments.description: Grip strength as a clinical trial endpoint. Remote, objective, defensible.
